events.title: Eastern Market — Weekend Market & Flea
events.description: Historic public market plus weekend outdoor flea, arts, and crafts. Live music when weather permits.

events.title: Capitol Hill Farmers Markets
events.description: Seasonal weekend farmers markets with local produce and vendors.

events.title: Lincoln Park
events.description: Large Capitol Hill green space with playground, paths, and statues. Dog-friendly. Cafes at both ends.

events.title: Garfield Park
events.description: Neighborhood park with playground, tennis, basketball, bocce, and a community garden.

events.title: Yards Park & Navy Yard Waterfront
events.description: Riverfront park with fountains, boardwalk, and frequent seasonal weekend programming.

events.title: National Mall & Smithsonian Museums
events.description: Free Smithsonian museums and monuments. Reliable all-weather, all-ages option.

events.title: United States Botanic Garden
events.description: Free conservatory and gardens at the foot of the Capitol, with a Children's Garden.

events.title: Anacostia Riverwalk Trail Ride
events.description: Paved riverfront trail for a family bike ride, with Capital Bikeshare access nearby.

events.title: The Fields at RFK — Community Open Play & Disc Golf
events.time: Open hours vary (8 AM–dusk disc golf)
events.venue: The Fields at RFK Campus (401 Oklahoma Ave NE)
events.description: Turf fields with community open-play hours plus DC's free disc golf course. 10-min walk from Stadium-Armory.

events.title: CHAW Gallery (Capitol Hill Arts Workshop)
events.venue: Capitol Hill Arts Workshop (545 7th St SE)
events.description: Rotating gallery shows of local artists, two blocks from Eastern Market. Check site for current exhibition.

events.title: National STEM Festival (Open Build Day / Public Days)
events.description: The National STEM Festival powered by EXPLR, an official America250 partner, runs June 22–27 and brings together the nation's top young minds to solve real problems. Hands-on celebration of youth innovation covering AI, aerospace, clean energy, biomedical research, and sustainable agriculture. Public Open Build Day is an all-ages expo celebrating the next generation of builders. Free admission.

events.title: A Fine Madness
events.venue: Woolly Mammoth Theatre + partner sites (Dupont Underground, Hamiltonian Artists, The Nicholson Project)
events.price: Check woollymammoth.net; Woolly offers pay-what-you-can and $20 under-30 pricing
events.description: Created and performed by Woolly company member Justin Weaks, A Fine Madness is a bold, deeply personal one-person show that transforms theater into a living archive. Performances tour across multiple DC venues from Woolly's Rehearsal Hall to Dupont Underground, Hamiltonian Artists, and The Nicholson Project.

events.title: Fest Wknd – R&B & Soca Block Party
events.description: Multi-day R&B and soca block party at Navy Yard. Each day has its own theme — live R&B hits, hip-swaying soca, '00s throwbacks, and more — with six artists and 12 DJs over three days.

events.title: Fest Wknd R&B & Soca Block Party
events.description: A multi-day R&B and soca block party in the Navy Yard neighborhood. Washingtonian calls it a standout for the last weekend of June.

events.title: Giant National Capital BBQ Battle
events.time: Saturday 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM; Sunday 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
events.venue: Pennsylvania Avenue NW, between 3rd and 7th Streets NW
events.description: America's official National BBQ Championship (recognized by Congress) transforms Pennsylvania Avenue into a culinary street party. World-class pitmasters compete for a $50,000 prize purse, six live music stages host rock, salsa, reggae, go-go, and blues acts, and a Giant Sampling Pavilion offers 100+ food samples. Kids zone features activities with Wizards, Capitals, Mystics, Nationals, and D.C. United mascots. Proceeds benefit Capital Area Food Bank and DC Central Kitchen.

events.title: Sally & Tom – Shakespeare Theatre Company
events.venue: Sidney Harman Hall, 610 F Street NW
events.description: Pulitzer Prize-winning playwright Suzan-Lori Parks delivers a sharp, layered new play in which a scrappy theater company stages a show about Sally Hemings and Thomas Jefferson. Running through June 28, this is a must-see for the final weekend of June.

events.title: Suffs
events.venue: The National Theatre, 1321 Pennsylvania Ave NW
events.price: Broadway touring prices; avg ~$200+. TodayTix rush/lottery likely available.
events.description: The Tony Award-winning musical about the women's suffrage movement — 'Behind every powerful woman… are more powerful women.' National Broadway tour stop at The National Theatre for a two-week limited engagement.

events.title: Jazz in the Garden — National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.venue: National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.description: A beloved free outdoor jazz concert series at the National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden, running all summer on Friday evenings from 5–8:30pm. Bring a blanket, stake out a spot on the grass, and enjoy live jazz among the sculptures. Food and drinks available for purchase. Note: June 27 and July 3 Fridays fall within the target weekends. Check NGA's site for the specific lineup each week.

events.title: National Gallery of Art — Jazz in the Garden (Summer Series)
events.venue: National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.description: DC's beloved outdoor Jazz in the Garden series runs all summer on Fridays from 5–8:30 p.m. at the NGA Sculpture Garden. This season's theme is 'American Sounds,' a nod to the nation's 250th anniversary. Free to attend; food and beverages available for purchase. Note: ticket lottery opens the week prior on Monday at 10 a.m. and closes Friday at noon.

events.title: Smithsonian Civic Season — Daily Drop-In Programs at American History Museum
events.description: In collaboration with Made By Us (a national coalition engaging young people in history), the National Museum of American History hosts daily drop-in programs noon–4 PM, June 19–July 4, connected to its 'In Pursuit of Life, Liberty & Happiness' exhibition. Features 250 artifacts spanning 1700s to the 21st century, with curators and community leaders available for informal conversation. Part of the nation's 250th anniversary celebration.

events.title: Teens Sip & Make — Drop-In Creative Space (MLK Library)
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library (Central)
events.description: Recurring drop-in creative space for teens at MLK Memorial Library during Discover Summer. Part of the broader Discover Summer programming offering hands-on arts and crafts activities for teens. Also look for 'Teen Lounge' hang-out sessions, Teen Movie Monday, Teen Anime Club, and Youth Chess at various branches.

events.title: The Two Georges: Parallel Lives in an Age of Revolution (Library of Congress)
events.time: During Library of Congress open hours
events.description: The Library of Congress brings together letters, ledgers, diaries, maps, drawings and artifacts illuminating the similarities and differences between George Washington and King George III — two iconic Revolutionary War figures who never met. The exhibit features items from the Royal Archives at Windsor Castle, London's Science Museum, and George Washington's Mount Vernon.

events.title: Capitol Riverfront Friday Night Concert Series at Yards Park (14th Season)
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk Stage (355 Water Street SE)
events.description: Free weekly outdoor concert series in its 14th season on the Yards Park boardwalk along the Anacostia River. Every Friday night of summer features fantastic live bands with river views, food and beverages nearby, and family-friendly open grassy space. Specific band lineups for June 20, June 27, and July 3–4 weekend dates not yet published; check capitolriverfront.org for updates.

events.title: Capitol Riverfront Saturday Farmers Market at Half Street
events.venue: Half Street SE, Capitol Riverfront / Navy Yard
events.description: Weekly Saturday farmers market in the Capitol Riverfront neighborhood on Half Street. Fresh produce, local vendors, and community gathering every Saturday morning from 9am–1pm through the summer.

events.title: Eastern Market Weekend Outdoor Arts & Farmers Market
events.description: Historic Eastern Market's weekend outdoor market features local farmers offering farm-fresh produce, plus artisans and crafters with handmade arts and crafts from around the world. Open Tuesday–Sunday with the largest outdoor footprint on weekends. A community hub for 152 years in the heart of Capitol Hill.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Watch Zone at The Wharf – Weekend Matches
events.description: Daily World Cup match screenings on the big screen at Pearl Street. The June 20 weekend features matches including Portugal vs. Uzbekistan, England vs. Ghana, and Panama vs. Croatia. The June 27 weekend features Switzerland vs. Canada, Scotland vs. Brazil, and Czechia vs. Mexico. Runs through the tournament finale on July 19. Special Team USA watch parties (June 19, June 25) include expanded sip-and-stroll zones and giveaways.

events.title: FIFA World Cup Fan Zone – National Mall
events.time: Open as late as 1 AM for select games
events.venue: National Mall, between 3rd and 4th Streets NW (near U.S. Capitol)
events.description: Official FIFA World Cup 2026 fan zone on the National Mall showing all Team USA matches and dozens of other games including every knockout-round match. Features live match viewing on big screens, interactive exhibits, youth programming, cultural showcases, food, music, and family-friendly activations. Free and open to the public.

events.title: How Shakespeare Saved My Life
events.venue: Folger Shakespeare Library, 201 East Capitol St SE
events.price: Tickets from $20; 50% discount for public servants with code THANKYOU
events.description: World premiere solo show written and performed by Broadway star Jacob Ming-Trent, directed by Tony Taccone. An autobiographical dive into the playwright's turbulent youth, blending Shakespeare with the voices of Basquiat, Biggie, and Tupac. Co-produced by Berkeley Repertory Theatre, Folger Theatre, and Red Bull Theater. Run time: 90 minutes.

events.title: July 4th Spectacular on Pennsylvania Avenue
events.description: The July 4th Spectacular on Penn Ave brings the city and nation together over two days to commemorate America's 250th anniversary. Pennsylvania Avenue fills with interactive and immersive art, cultural activities, stunning light projections on iconic structures, large-format live art exhibits, and on-stage performers. Families can enjoy games and hands-on creative projects. Free and open to the public.

events.title: National Archives Extended Summer Hours (Declaration of Independence & Bill of Rights)
events.time: Extended summer hours (closed June 23); check archives.gov
events.description: The National Archives extends its summer hours June 22 through July 5 (except June 23), giving visitors more time to see the Declaration of Independence, the Constitution, and the Bill of Rights — especially relevant during the nation's 250th anniversary and July 4th weekend. Free admission.

events.title: The Wharf Farmers Market (M Street Landing) – Saturdays
events.description: Weekly Saturday farmers market at M Street Landing at The Wharf, supporting local farmers offering locally sourced food. Started April 4, 2026 and runs through the season. Located alongside DC's oldest continuously operating open-air fish market.

events.title: Great American State Fair
events.venue: National Mall (U.S. Capitol to Washington Monument)
events.description: A once-in-a-generation, 16-day World's Fair-scale national exposition spanning from the U.S. Capitol to the Washington Monument, celebrating America's 250th birthday. All 56 U.S. states and territories showcase food, culture, and pride. Features live music, carnival rides, and dozens of hands-on activations. Anchors the July 4th semiquincentennial weekend.

events.title: Great American State Fair (National Mall / America 250)
events.venue: National Mall (U.S. Capitol to Washington Monument)
events.description: A 16-day World's Fair-scale national exposition spanning the National Mall from the U.S. Capitol to the Washington Monument, running June 25–July 10. All 56 U.S. states and territories showcase their food, culture, and pride with live music, carnival rides, and dozens of hands-on activations. A once-in-a-generation America 250 celebration. Free with advance registration.

events.title: Feeling Afraid As If Something Terrible Is Going to Happen
events.price: Studio Theatre tickets typically $45–$95; under-30 and rush pricing available
events.description: A new play running at Studio Theatre through mid-July 2026. Studio Theatre is known for intimate, thought-provoking productions. Under-30 and TodayTix rush tickets are typically available for Studio shows.

events.title: Beetlejuice
events.venue: The National Theatre, 1321 Pennsylvania Ave NW
events.price: Broadway touring prices; avg ~$200+. TodayTix rush/lottery likely available.
events.description: IT'S SHOWTIME! The wildly popular Broadway musical comedy based on the Tim Burton film returns on national tour. Beetlejuice is a musical comedy about making the most of every day-o — ghosts, the afterlife, and plenty of laughs.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zone — National Mall
events.time: Opens 1 hour before first match shown; closes 1 hour after last match. Zone open until midnight on July 4.
events.venue: National Mall (between 3rd & 4th Streets NW, near U.S. Capitol)
events.description: The free FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Zone on the National Mall runs the full length of the tournament (June 11–July 19). Live match viewing on a giant Jumbotron, interactive exhibits, youth soccer programming, cultural showcases, international food, and family-friendly activities. All Team USA matches shown regardless of time; starting July 4 (Round of 16), every match is shown. Zone stays open until midnight on July 4.

events.title: American Aspirations Exhibition — Smithsonian Castle
events.description: Co-curated by Smithsonian Secretary Lonnie G. Bunch, American Aspirations brings together the Smithsonian's most treasured objects to commemorate America's 250th, including Jefferson's writing desk used for the Declaration of Independence, Sally Ride's in-space flight suit, Harriet Tubman's personal hymnal, and George Washington's candle stand. The Smithsonian Castle is temporarily open through Sept. 7.

events.title: Pippin
events.venue: Signature Theatre (The Max), 4200 Campbell Ave, Arlington, VA
events.price: Signature single tickets typically $40–$100+; rush and under-30 pricing may apply
events.description: Stephen Schwartz and Roger O. Hirson's beloved musical follows young Pippin on his search for meaning, magic, and his 'corner of the sky.' Signature Theatre's production runs through late July 2026 in Arlington, VA.

events.title: CrazySexyCool – The TLC Musical
events.price: Tickets from ~$45; TodayTix rush/lottery may apply
events.description: A musical celebrating the iconic R&B group TLC, tracing the rise of Tionne 'T-Boz' Watkins, Lisa 'Left Eye' Lopes, and Rozonda 'Chilli' Thomas from scrappy newcomers to one of the best-selling female groups of all time.

events.title: CrazySexyCool: The TLC Musical at Arena Stage
events.description: A new production reliving the music of '90s supergroup TLC, playing through August 9 at Arena Stage in Southwest DC. Opened mid-June to strong interest.

events.title: The Fruits of Our Labor
events.venue: Arena Stage (Fichandler Stage / Kreeger Theatre), 1101 6th St SW
events.price: Tickets from ~$45; TodayTix rush/lottery may apply
events.description: A world-premiere production from Step Afrika! commemorating America's 250th anniversary. Directed by Jakari Sherman, this dance-theater work blends step performance with storytelling to celebrate the labor and legacy that built the nation.

events.title: Discover Summer for Teens — Know Your Power Art Contest
events.time: Submit anytime; pick up entry forms at any branch
events.description: Teens ages 13–19 are invited to submit an original work of writing, photography, film, visual art, or music expressing their feelings on a social issue that matters to them. Winners selected in four categories: Visual Art, Writing, Music, and Film & Photography. Judged by Pepco and DCPL's Teen Council.

events.title: Capitol Riverfront Friday Night Concert Series
events.description: The free Capitol Riverfront Friday Night Concert Series is back for its 9th season. Every Friday night this summer, head to Yards Park for live bands, river views, food and beverages, and easy access to nearby restaurants. Covers the June 20 and June 27 weekends (Friday kickoff nights).

events.title: Festival Grounds at RFK Campus – Summer Season (Open for Permitted Events)
events.venue: Festival Grounds at RFK Campus (2500 Independence Avenue SE)
events.description: DC's largest open-air venue on the banks of the Anacostia River. The summer season permit window runs June 14–August 29, 2026, hosting festivals, concerts, carnivals, and community events. Check eventsdc.com for specific ticketed events on target weekends.

events.title: THE PLAYGROUND at National Building Museum
events.venue: National Building Museum, 401 F Street NW
events.description: Created in collaboration with design firm Snarkitecture, THE PLAYGROUND transforms the National Building Museum's iconic Great Hall into an immersive environment for climbing, building, exploring, gathering, and imaginative play across nine unique activation areas. Open July 3–August 30; also features Late Night events, Hard Hat Tours, and workshops. Opens just in time for the July 4th weekend.

events.title: DCPL Teen/Tween Book Club — MLK Library
events.time: Varies; check dclibrary.libnet.info for upcoming dates
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library (Central)
events.description: The MLK Teen/Tween Book Club meets during Discover Summer at the Central Library. A summer 2026 session featuring Lumberjanes by N.D. Stevenson et al. was confirmed in DCPL event listings. Check the DCPL events calendar for exact Saturday/weekend dates.

events.title: Discover Summer — All-Ages Reading & Learning Program (DCPL System-Wide)
events.description: DC Public Library's all-ages summer celebration of reading and learning runs June 1–Aug. 31 across every branch. Kids and teens pick up Bingo Boards (or sign up on Beanstack), attend drop-in events, and win prizes from the Washington Nationals and D.C. United. Teens (13–19) can also enter the Know Your Power Art Contest — submitting writing, photography, film, visual art, or music on a social issue — through Aug. 12.

events.title: Summer Science Squad — STEAM Drop-In (MLK Library)
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library — Youth Program Room (204-F)
events.description: Hands-on STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Math) programming for kids and tweens at MLK Library as part of Discover Summer. Drop-in format confirmed in DCPL's summer event listings.

events.title: National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden — Extended Summer Hours
events.venue: National Gallery of Art Sculpture Garden
events.description: The National Gallery of Art's Sculpture Garden extends its hours through summer (June 3–Sept. 5), giving families and teens more time to enjoy free outdoor sculpture on the National Mall. Completely free, no tickets required.

events.title: District Vibes / American Pride Exhibition
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library, 901 G Street NW
events.description: DC gave the world the football huddle, the modern blood bank, and legends like Marvin Gaye. This free exhibition at MLK Library — with satellite locations across the city — chronicles the vibrant contributions of Washingtonians through images, artifacts, storytelling, and free programs through September 27.

events.title: District Vibes / American Pride: How D.C. Changed American Culture (MLK Library Exhibit)
events.venue: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library (Central)
events.description: A public art and culture exhibit at MLK Memorial Library exploring D.C.'s outsized influence on American culture, running June 3 through September 27, 2026. Free to view during regular library hours.

events.title: America's State Flowers: An America250 Celebration (US Botanic Garden)
events.description: The US Botanic Garden showcases the official flowers of all 50 states, D.C., and U.S. territories across the Conservatory and outdoor gardens — with living plants, botanical illustrations, herbarium specimens, and intricate paper flower sculptures by artist Emily Paluska. A scavenger hunt format makes it especially fun for families and teens. Associated drop-in demonstrations and workshops run throughout the exhibit. The Garden also has a Children's Garden open in 2026. Free, no tickets needed.

events.title: Fierce Flora: Tales of Survival and Demise (US Botanic Garden)
events.description: A multisensory exhibit at the USBG exploring the ferocious side of the plant world — carnivorous plants, toxic species, and the evolutionary survival strategies plants use against animals and competitors. Highlights that many pharmaceutical drugs are derived from plants. Free with regular Garden admission.

events.title: Lincoln Memorial Undercroft – Public Opening
events.description: For the first time, the public can explore the Undercroft beneath the Lincoln Memorial — floor-to-ceiling glass reveals the grid of concrete columns that uphold the iconic structure, plus an interactive exhibit space exploring the memorial's construction history and enduring significance. Opens June 25 as part of America 250 celebrations.

events.title: Capital Pride Parade
events.venue: Pennsylvania Avenue NW (Dupont Circle to downtown)
events.description: The annual Capital Pride Parade winds through Dupont Circle and downtown DC, featuring elaborate floats, drag performers, marching bands, local organizations, and corporations. One of DC's most vibrant and attended annual festivals for the LGBTQ+ community.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy & More
events.description: Weekend showcase headlined by Patrice DeVeaux, with Shaun Murphy, Christian Escoto, and Joey Duffield. Part of the Jun 19–21 run at DC's premier comedy club. Approximately 90 minutes.

events.title: Everyday People Day Party at The Bullpen
events.description: A groovy day party at The Bullpen in Navy Yard. Washingtonian describes it as a 'groovy option for a day party.'

events.title: Everyday People Day Party at The Bullpen
events.description: Groovy outdoor DJ day party at The Bullpen in Navy Yard, featuring R&B, hip-hop, and more genres.

events.title: Fête de la Musique Georgetown
events.description: Georgetown hosts several live acts and concerts as an ode to the French holiday Fête de la Musique, a worldwide celebration of music on the summer solstice.

events.title: Home Rule Music Festival
events.venue: The Great Lawn, 1301 Main Drive NW (Walter Reed Campus)
events.description: A DC-centric festival celebrating the city's rich music and cultural scene. Lineup includes Sun Ra Arkestra featuring Marshall Allen, Chuck Brown Band, Trouble Funk, and Melanie Charles. Features live music across jazz, go-go, soul and hip-hop, a curated Music & Record Fair with local record shops, live visual art and mural painting, a Kids Zone, local food vendors, and a Youth Showcase opening the festival.

events.title: Home Rule Music Festival
events.description: DC takes center stage at the Home Rule Music Festival, a one-day culture and music festival featuring local artists playing go-go, punk, indie, and more at venues across the city. Tickets vary; some events are free.

events.title: Home Rule Music Festival
events.venue: The Great Lawn, Walter Reed Campus, 1301 Main Drive NW
events.description: DC-centric festival celebrating the city's rich music and culture. Lineup includes Sun Ra Arkestra featuring Marshall Allen, Chuck Brown Band, Trouble Funk, and Melanie Charles. Features live music across genres (jazz, go-go, soul, hip-hop), a Music & Record Fair with local record shops, live visual art, mural painting, a Kids Zone, local food vendors, and a Youth Showcase where student musicians open the festival.

events.title: Rock the Dock Free Summer Concerts – Grooves in the Grove Summer Series (Saturdays)
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: Free Friday-night concert series on Transit Pier presented by Pacifico Beer. The July 3 edition (closest weekend date) features Country Line Dance Night with DC Rawhides and music by Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service. All ages welcome; family-friendly.

events.title: Sally's Night at the National Air and Space Museum
events.description: Sally's Night returns to the Air and Space Museum to celebrate Sally Ride's legacy as the first American woman in space. Visitors can do a digital scavenger hunt through the museum, design a mission patch or write a postcard, and visit Discovery Stations about Ride and women in STEM. Free, but tickets are required in advance.

events.title: Smithsonian Solstice Weekend — All Nat'l Mall Museums Open Until 11 PM
events.time: Open until 11:00 PM (Friday June 19 and Saturday June 20)
events.price: Free (some programs require free timed passes)
events.description: The Smithsonian expands its Solstice Saturday into a three-day celebration (June 19–21) marking the start of summer and America's 250th anniversary. All National Mall museums stay open until 11 PM on Friday June 19 and Saturday June 20, with special after-hours programming. Includes the Anacostia Community Museum (open until 10 PM on Saturday), off-Mall locations, and Science Solstice Sunday at the National Zoo on June 21. Some events require advance free passes.

events.title: Solstice Celebration at the National Museum of African Art
events.venue: National Museum of African Art, Smithsonian
events.description: Stay out late at the National Museum of African Art for a Solstice Celebration featuring curator-led tours of 'Here: Pride and Belonging in African Art' (6:30 & 7:30 p.m.), plus DJ sets and live music with DJ Detailz, Plus Ultra Entertainment, and Ni Dembaya Band (8:30–11:30 p.m.).

events.title: Tim Meadows Live – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: Emmy-winning SNL alumnus Tim Meadows performs at the Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a Jun 18–20 run at this art-deco dine-in comedy theater. Full bar/restaurant menu served at your seat.

events.title: Capital Pride Festival & Concert
events.venue: Pennsylvania Avenue NW, between 3rd and 7th Streets NW
events.description: The culminating day of Capital Pride features a free outdoor festival and concert on Pennsylvania Avenue with nationally known LGBTQ+ artists, dance performances, and speeches from activists. The weekend wraps up America's largest pride celebration for DC's LGBTQ+ community.

events.title: D.C. United FIFA World Cup Watch Party – Tingey Plaza
events.venue: Tingey Plaza SE, 101 Tingey St SE
events.description: D.C. United's 'United in Play' Soccer Celebration hosts an official FIFA World Cup 2026 watch party at Tingey Plaza in Navy Yard. Free event with DJs, live music, food trucks, and community programming. Part of a three-day celebration (June 19–21). Registration required.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy & More (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of the Jun 19–21 showcase run featuring Patrice DeVeaux, Shaun Murphy, Christian Escoto, and Joey Duffield at DC's most famous comedy stage.

events.title: Father's Day Rose Remembrance at the Vietnam Veterans Memorial
events.description: Every Father's Day, the Vietnam Veterans Memorial Fund lays thousands of roses at The Wall in honor of the fathers commemorated there — those who never came home and those who later passed. Each rose carries a personal message. A moving, free public commemoration on the National Mall.

events.title: Flawed Mangoes: The Killswitch Memories Tour
events.description: Flawed Mangoes bring their Killswitch Memories Tour to Union Stage on Sunday evening.

events.title: Science Solstice Sunday at the National Zoo
events.description: Science Solstice Sunday kicks off summer with a high-energy, hands-on experience for all ages at the National Zoo. An interactive scavenger hunt leads participants across the zoo grounds, while live animal demonstrations and activity stations spotlight the Smithsonian's conservation science — from elephant research to genetics, ecosystems, and space.

events.title: Dauood Naimyar – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Stand-up comedian Dauood Naimyar headlines at Arlington's dine-in comedy theater. Part of a multi-show run at the Drafthouse.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Joey Friedman, Chris Alan, Matt Deakins & More (Friday)
events.description: Weekend showcase featuring Joey Friedman, Chris Alan, Matt Deakins, Caitlin McDevitt, and Matt Brown. Part of the Jun 25–26 run.

events.title: Navy Yard Friday Night Concert Series – Hi Fi! Soul Band
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water Street SE
events.description: Free outdoor concert on the Yards Park Boardwalk featuring Hi Fi! Soul Band, performing a wide-ranging soul and R&B set covering Beyoncé, Stevie Wonder, Earth Wind & Fire, Bruno Mars, and more. Family-friendly with open grassy space along the Anacostia riverfront.

events.title: Carmen Lynch – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: Carmen Lynch — known for appearances on The Tonight Show, The Late Show with Colbert, and Letterman — headlines at Arlington's dine-in comedy theater. Multi-show run over the Jun 27 weekend.

events.title: Red Bull Spin Off
events.description: Red Bull Spin Off challenges teams of two to build their own bicycles and race them across a floating racetrack on the Potomac at The Wharf. Watch teams race creative, flashy bikes and explore the Wharf's restaurants, bars, and shops around the spectacle. Free admission, first-come first-served seating.

events.title: Tara Cannistraci – DC Comedy Loft
events.description: Stand-up headliner Tara Cannistraci performs at the DC Comedy Loft, located at 1523 22nd St NW. Multi-show weekend engagement.

events.title: The Illharmonic – Black Music Month
events.description: The Illharmonic combines Hip-Hop with orchestral accompaniment for a Black Music Month performance at Lincoln Theatre.

events.title: WABA Bike to the BBQ Battle
events.description: A 10-mile community group ride through the heart of DC organized by WABA, DC Bike Ride, and Streets Calling Bike Club, ending at the 34th Annual Giant National Capital BBQ Battle on Pennsylvania Avenue. The ride-only option is free with refreshments at the WABA rest stop. The $15 Ride + Festival pass includes skip-the-line festival entry, an exclusive t-shirt, mid-ride refreshments, and free secure bike valet parking at the festival.

events.title: Himra
events.description: Himra performs at Union Stage on Sunday evening.

events.title: Michael Henry & Tim Murray – DC Comedy Loft (Sunday)
events.description: Comedian duo Michael Henry & Tim Murray headline the DC Comedy Loft on Sunday. Multi-show format at this intimate West End venue.

events.title: Tony Woods – DC Improv
events.description: Veteran stand-up comedian Tony Woods performs at DC Improv on Sunday evening. One-night engagement.

events.title: Sunset Cinema – 'National Treasure: Book of Secrets' (Free Outdoor Movie)
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: Free Wednesday outdoor movie series on Transit Pier presented by Pacifico Beer. July 1 screening is National Treasure: Book of Secrets (2007). Stageside kiosk Union Pie offers pizza; Cantina Bambina serves Pacifico Beer.

events.title: Wharf Sunset Cinema – 'National Treasure: Book of Secrets'
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf, 970 Wharf St SW
events.description: Free weekly Wednesday outdoor movie series at The Wharf's Transit Pier, presented by Pacifico Beer. On July 1, the screening is 'National Treasure: Book of Secrets' (2007) — perfect pre-July 4th patriotic viewing. No tickets needed; arrive early for a seat. Food and drinks from Wharf restaurants available.

events.title: Navy Yard Friday Night Concert Series – SALTY (July 3 Release Party)
events.venue: Yards Park Boardwalk, 355 Water Street SE
events.description: Free outdoor concert on the Yards Park Boardwalk featuring SALTY in a high-energy waterfront performance that doubles as the official release party for their new single 'Ibiza Skies.' Includes an exclusive vinyl giveaway for fans. Family-friendly along the Anacostia riverfront.

events.title: Rock the Dock Free Concert – Country Line Dance Night (July 3)
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: July 3 Rock the Dock concert features Country Line Dance Night with DC Rawhides and music by Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service. Free, all-ages, on Transit Pier with Pacifico Beer available waterside.

events.title: Rock the Dock Free Summer Concert – Country Line Dance Night (DC Rawhides & Shawn Parsons)
events.venue: Transit Pier at The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: Free Friday night outdoor concert on the Transit Pier featuring a Country Line Dance Night with DC Rawhides and live music by Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service. Pre-Fourth of July waterfront celebration. Food from any Wharf restaurant permitted.

events.title: A Capitol Fourth Concert
events.venue: West Lawn, U.S. Capitol, Capitol Hill
events.description: The iconic 90-minute nationally broadcast Independence Day concert on the West Lawn of the U.S. Capitol, featuring the National Symphony Orchestra, major vocalists, and military bands. Part of the America 250th semiquincentennial celebration. Bring blankets and low chairs; the lawn opens in the late afternoon.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Patrice DeVeaux, Ross Benoit, Justo & More (July 4th Weekend)
events.description: Holiday weekend showcase at DC Improv featuring Patrice DeVeaux, Ross Benoit, Justo, Jack Coleman, and David Balagizi. Jul 3 lineup confirmed — check for Jul 4–5 continuation shows.

events.title: Fourth at The Wharf – Free Live Music & Fireworks Viewing (America 250)
events.venue: District Pier, Transit Pier & The Grove, The Wharf
events.description: Free America 250 celebration with live country music on multiple outdoor stages: District Pier Stage (Melissa Quinn Fox at 5pm, Chris Ryan Band at 7pm), Transit Pier Floating Stage (Shane Gamble & The Pawnshop Hearts at 5pm, Shawn Parsons & The Secret Service at 7pm), The Grove Stage (Jeremiah Prophett & Band at 7pm), and M Street Landing (Gary Palumbo at 5pm, Emily Woodhull at 7pm). Spectacular views of NPS fireworks launched from the National Mall. VIP Dockmaster Building experience available at $60+; must be 21+ to consume alcohol. All ages welcome.

events.title: July 4th at the National Archives
events.description: The only place in the world to view the original Declaration of Independence hosts a special July 4th celebration for America's 250th. Greet George and Martha Washington re-enactors, enjoy family-fun activities, sign a facsimile of the Declaration, and see the founding documents. The Archives is running extended hours June 22–July 5 for the semiquincentennial.

events.title: National Independence Day Parade
events.venue: Constitution Avenue NW, from 7th Street to 17th Street
events.description: America's biggest Independence Day parade kicks off at 11:45 AM on Constitution Avenue, expanding in 2026 to honor the 250th anniversary with floats, military units, marching bands, fife and drum corps, equestrian teams, and cultural organizations from all 50 states.

events.title: Salute to America – 250th Fireworks Celebration
events.venue: National Mall (fireworks launched from Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool area)
events.description: The 'Salute to America: The Nation's 250th Birthday Fireworks Celebration' national broadcast combines headline music performances, military flyovers, and speeches, culminating in a fireworks finale launched from both sides of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. This is the emotional peak of the entire semiquincentennial. Free viewing from the National Mall.

events.title: Front Royal
events.description: Front Royal plays Pearl Street Warehouse on the Sunday of July 4th weekend. Free RSVP event, all ages.

events.title: Sunset Cinema – 'Hamilton' (Free Outdoor Movie)
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf (970 Wharf Street SW)
events.description: Free outdoor movie series on Transit Pier. July 8 screens the filmed Broadway production of Hamilton (2020). Part of The Wharf's all-summer Sunset Cinema series presented by Pacifico Beer.

events.title: Wharf Sunset Cinema – 'Hamilton'
events.venue: Transit Pier, The Wharf, 970 Wharf St SW
events.description: Free weekly Wednesday outdoor movie series at The Wharf's Transit Pier. On July 8, the screening is the filmed stage production of 'Hamilton' (2020) — a perfect post-July 4th patriotic viewing on the waterfront. No tickets needed; arrive early for seating. Food and drinks from Wharf restaurants available.

events.title: Andrew Orolfo – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Stand-up comedian Andrew Orolfo headlines at the Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a multi-show run at this dine-in comedy theater in Arlington, VA.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman & More (Saturday)
events.description: Weekend showcase headlined by Stacey Axler with Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman, and Ty Davis. Part of the Jul 9–12 run at DC Improv.

events.title: Escape with Washington Improv Theater – Saturday Matinee
events.venue: Washington Improv Theater at Studio Theatre
events.description: WIT's long-form improv show 'Escape' — an audience-driven, spontaneous performance by Washington Improv Theater's experienced ensembles at Studio Theatre on 14th St. All-ages welcome.

events.title: DC Improv Showcase: Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman & More (Sunday)
events.description: Final day of the Jul 9–12 showcase featuring Stacey Axler, Lafayette Wright, Blake Freeman, and Ty Davis at DC Improv.

events.title: US Botanic Garden — Summer Evening Hours with Live Music (July Edition)
events.description: The USBG stays open until 8 p.m. each third Thursday in June, July, August, and September 2026, offering golden-hour and sunset viewing amid summer flowers and the America's State Flowers exhibit, plus live music. July features Night Hawk, an American indie rock band with violin, finger-style guitar, and indie/alternative rock. (Note: July 16 is the nearest such evening to the July 4 weekend.)

events.title: USBG Summer Evening Hours with Live Music
events.description: The US Botanic Garden hosts special extended evening hours on the third Thursday of each summer month (June 18, July 16, Aug. 20, Sept. 17). The July 16 session features Night Hawk, an American indie rock band with violin, finger-style guitar, and indie/alternative rock energy. Explore the America's State Flowers exhibit at golden hour while enjoying live music. Lemonade, botanical mocktails, snacks, and ice cream available for purchase. Free entry; no tickets required.

events.title: Lewis Belt – DC Improv Lounge Showroom (Friday)
events.description: Oakland-born stand-up comedian, actor, and producer Lewis Belt performs in DC Improv's intimate 60-seat Lounge Showroom. Part of a multi-show run through the weekend of Jul 18.

events.title: Rich Vos – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse (Friday)
events.description: Veteran stand-up comedian Rich Vos headlines at Arlington Drafthouse. Part of a multi-show run at this dine-in theater on Columbia Pike.

events.title: Audrey Stewart – Arlington Cinema & Drafthouse
events.description: LA-based stand-up comedian and actress Audrey Stewart headlines at Arlington Drafthouse. A regular at the Hollywood Improv and Laugh Factory, performing her stand-up set in a full dine-in environment.

events.title: Chris D'Elia – DC Improv (Saturday)
events.description: Nationally touring headliner Chris D'Elia performs at DC Improv as part of the Jul 17–19 engagement. D'Elia is known for his popular podcast 'Congratulations.'

events.title: Chris D'Elia – DC Improv (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of Chris D'Elia's Jul 17–19 engagement at DC Improv. Check the venue for specific showtime and ticket availability.

events.title: Sheryl Underwood – DC Improv (Saturday)
events.description: Emmy Award-winning comedian and co-host of 'The Talk' Sheryl Underwood headlines DC Improv as part of her Jul 24–26 engagement.

events.title: Sheryl Underwood – DC Improv (Sunday)
events.description: Final night of Sheryl Underwood's Jul 24–26 run at DC Improv. Underwood is the Emmy-winning co-host of the CBS daytime show 'The Talk.'
